evals.name: declarative-crud-client
evals.prompt: Create a declarative HTTP client for the User API in a Kora application. The client must have the methods: getUser(id) GET /users/{id}, createUser(request) POST /users, updateUser(id, request) PUT /users/{id}, deleteUser(id) DELETE /users/{id}. Use @HttpClient, @HttpRoute, and @Json annotations. Method signatures must be synchronous.
evals.expected_output: Interface with @HttpClient("user-api"), @HttpRoute for CRUD operations, @Json for DTOs, synchronous return types

evals.name: imperative-client
evals.prompt: Create an imperative HTTP client for an External API. The client must use the HttpClient interface directly, have methods for GET/POST requests, and include basic error handling. Method signatures must return CompletionStage for asynchronous processing.
evals.expected_output: Class with @Component, HttpClient injected via @Tag, methods returning CompletionStage, manual HttpClientRequest construction

evals.name: basic-auth-interceptor
evals.prompt: Create an HttpClientInterceptor for Basic Authentication. The interceptor must add the Authorization: Basic <credentials> header to every request. Credentials must be Base64-encoded from username:password. Use CompletionStage for the method signature.
evals.expected_output: Class implementing HttpClientInterceptor, adding Authorization header, Base64 encoding, CompletionStage return type

evals.name: api-key-auth-interceptor
evals.prompt: Create an HttpClientInterceptor for API Key authentication. The interceptor must add a custom header (for example X-API-Key or Authorization: Bearer) containing the API key value. The header name and key value must be configurable via the constructor.
evals.expected_output: Class implementing HttpClientInterceptor, adding the API key header, flexible configuration via constructor

evals.name: bearer-token-interceptor
evals.prompt: Create an HttpClientInterceptor for Bearer Token authentication. The token must be obtained dynamically via Supplier<String> (to support token refresh). Add the Authorization: Bearer <token> header to every request.
evals.expected_output: Class implementing HttpClientInterceptor, Supplier<String> for the token, Authorization: Bearer header

evals.name: custom-response-mapper
evals.prompt: Create a custom HttpClientResponseMapper for XML format. The mapper must parse an XML response into a Java object and handle parsing errors. Use the HttpClientResponseMapper<T> interface.
evals.expected_output: Class implementing HttpClientResponseMapper, XML parsing, status code handling

evals.name: sealed-response
evals.prompt: Create a sealed interface for a type-safe API response with the variants: Success(data), NotFound(error), ValidationError(errors), ServerError(error). Show an example of using it in a declarative client.
evals.expected_output: Sealed interface/class with subtypes for different response variants, usage example in a client

evals.name: query-parameters-client
evals.prompt: Create a declarative client with methods that use @Query parameters. GET /search with the parameters: q (String, required), category (String, optional), limit (int, default 20), offset (int, default 0), sortBy (String, optional).
evals.expected_output: Interface with @Query annotations, required/optional parameters, synchronous signatures

evals.name: response-code-mapper
evals.prompt: Create a declarative client with @ResponseCodeMapper for handling different HTTP status codes. 200 -> SuccessResponse, 404 -> NotFoundException, 400 -> BadRequestException, 500 -> ServerException. Use @Json to parse the response body.
evals.expected_output: Interface with @ResponseCodeMapper annotations, mapping status codes to types/exceptions

evals.name: logging-interceptor
evals.prompt: Create an HttpClientInterceptor for logging requests and responses. Log: method, URI, headers (excluding sensitive ones), execution time, and response status code. Use @Tag to apply the interceptor to a specific client.
evals.expected_output: Class implementing HttpClientInterceptor, request/response logging, timing, @Tag with the client class

evals.name: header-parameters-client
evals.prompt: Create a declarative client with methods that use @Header parameters. POST /api/data with the headers: X-Request-ID (required), X-User-ID (optional), Accept (default application/json).
evals.expected_output: Interface with @Header annotations, required/optional headers, default values

evals.name: error-handling-client
evals.prompt: Create an imperative client with error handling. Handle: HttpClientException for HTTP errors, TimeoutException for timeouts, IOException for network errors. Log errors and wrap them in RuntimeException with a clear message.
evals.expected_output: Class with try-catch blocks for different exception types, logging, wrapping
